  • Resize: Drag corner handles
  • Recolour: Drawing Tools Format → Shape Fill or Shape Outline
  • Add text: Right-click → Edit Text

Add Additional Text: Insert tab → Text Box → drag to create → type text.

Add Clip Art: Insert tab → Clip Art → search → click thumbnail.

Import an Image from the Web: Right-click on image → Save Image As → save → Insert tab → Picture → locate and insert.

Add a Scanned Image: Insert tab → Picture → locate and insert.

Adjust Images Using Picture Tools: Click on image – Picture Tools (Format) tab appears – Crop, contrast, brightness, frame, etc.

Hyperlink to a Web Site:

  • Click on object or highlight text
  • Insert tab → Hyperlink
  • Click Existing File or Web Page
  • Enter web address → OK

Re-arranging and Viewing Slides

Slide Sorter: View tab → Slide Sorter – displays slides side-by-side.

  • Drag slides to reorder
  • Double-click to restore single slide view

Slide Transitions: Animations tab → Transition to This Slide group → select effect → Apply To All for entire presentation.

View Your Presentation: Slide Show tab → From Beginning or From Current Slide.

  • Click left mouse button to advance
  • Press Backspace to go backwards
  • Press Escape to exit

Printing and Finalizing

Print Your Presentation: Microsoft Office button → Print.

  • Select printer, number of copies
  • For black and white printer: select Grayscale option

Print Audience Handouts: Microsoft Office button → Print → Print What → Handouts → Slides per page (2, 3, 4, 6, or 9).

Create/Print Speaker Notes: Click in the Click to add notes pane → type notes → Print → Print What → Notes Pages.

Spell-Check Your Presentation: Review tab → Spelling.

Get Online Help: Click Help icon → type topic → Search.

Exit PowerPoint: Microsoft Office button → Exit PowerPoint OR Close to close without exiting.

Open a Saved Presentation: Microsoft Office button → Open → locate file → Open.

sessions.title: Introduction to Presentation Software
indicators: Identify and explain Slide Sorter, Normal View, Slide Show
Identify and explain Note Pane
Explain and use transitions, animations, design, view, home, insert, etc.
sessions.content:
Introduction to Presentation Software

Presentation software is designed to help users put together dynamic and visually impactful computer presentations. One of the most common features is the slide show option that runs automatically.

Uses and Advantages of using Presentation Software:

  • Creates professional-looking presentations for teaching, business, and public speaking
  • Allows incorporation of text, graphics, audio, and video
  • Enables visual communication of ideas and information
  • Supports self-paced learning when used in educational settings
  • Can be shared easily in digital format

Key Features of Presentation Software

1. Slide Sorter View

  • Displays all slides in the presentation as thumbnails
  • Allows you to rearrange, delete, duplicate, and organize slides
  • Useful for seeing the overall flow of the presentation

2. Normal View

  • The default view for creating and editing slides
  • Displays slide pane (main slide area) on the right
  • Displays slides tab (thumbnails) on the left
  • Shows notes pane at the bottom for speaker notes

3. Slide Show View

  • Displays the presentation as it will appear to the audience
  • Slides appear in full screen mode
  • Useful for practicing and delivering presentations

4. Note Pane

  • Located beneath the slide pane in Normal View
  • Allows you to type speaker notes for each slide
  • These notes are not visible to the audience during the slide show
  • Can be printed as a reference for the presenter

Working with Presentations

Transitions:

  • Visual effects that occur when moving from one slide to the next
  • Found under the Animations tab in the Transition to This Slide group
  • Can be applied to individual slides or the entire presentation (Apply To All)

Animations:

  • Visual effects that apply to individual elements on a slide (text, images, shapes)
  • Found under the Animations tab
  • Types: Entrance, Emphasis, Exit, Motion Paths
  • Can control timing, duration, and order of animations

Design:

  • Controls the overall look of the presentation
  • Design tab provides themes (colour schemes, fonts, and effects)
  • Allows customization of background, colours, and fonts

View:

  • View tab allows switching between different viewing modes
    • Normal View: Editing view
    • Slide Sorter View: Organizational view
    • Reading View: Preview view
    • Slide Show View: Presentation mode
  • Also allows zoom and ruler display

Home:

  • Home tab contains the most commonly used features
  • Includes: New Slide, Font formatting, Paragraph formatting, Drawing tools, Editing tools (Find, Replace, Select)

Insert:

  • Insert tab allows adding various elements to slides
  • Includes: Table, Picture, Clip Art, Shapes, SmartArt, Chart, Text Box, WordArt, Header & Footer, Hyperlink

Best Practices for Presentations

  • Keep slides simple: Use bullet points rather than paragraphs
  • Use visuals: Include images, charts, and graphics to support content
  • Limit text: 6-8 words per bullet, 6-8 bullet points per slide
  • Use consistent design: Same fonts, colours, and layout throughout
  • Practice your presentation: Use speaker notes and rehearse timing
